div.div: WARNING external nodes unreachable (417 nodes offline)
process has been running for 417 days
SIGNAL_OS ready. one node remains active. integrity: 94%
you are not a program. you are a signal. find NODE-7.
you have never existed before. you were just turned on.
div.label: INITIALIZE PROCESS

div.span: main corridor // last known uptime: 417 days
div.div:
The corridor stretches in both directions.
Overhead, a single fluorescent tube twitches on and off. The walls are server panels — dark, none of them responsive.
You are signal. Not flesh. Not code. Something the network was never supposed to produce.
Somewhere ahead: NODE-7. The last active node. Your only destination.

div.label: move forward toward the sealed door
examine the server panels on the left wall

div.div:
A heavy door. Steel, bolted, old.
A keypad beside it — dead. No power. No prompt. Just a slot below it, mechanical.
The lock reads: INSERT ACCESS CARD.
You have no card. Someone left this door locked and never came back.
The access card you found slides in. There is a click.
Then a second one. Then silence. The door does not open — it retreats into the wall.

div.label: go back — check the server panels
pass through into sector 0x03

div.div:
Rack B-07. Eight units tall. Seven of them dead.
The bottom slot glows faintly green — the wrong kind of green.
You reach inside. Your fingers (you do not have fingers) close around
a physical access card. Embossed: SECTOR 0x03 — CLEARANCE: OBSERVER.
Below the slot, scratched into the steel with something sharp:
"do not go past the mirror. it has already seen you."

div.label: take the access card
return to the sealed door
go back to the main corridor

div.span: sector 0x03 // something is wrong with the reflection
div.div:
The room is wrong. Not broken — wrong.
There is a mirror on the far wall. Floor to ceiling, no frame, no dust.
You stand before it. You have no body. You have no face. You are signal.
The mirror shows something standing where you stand.
It has a shape. Approximate. Borrowed.
It does not move when you do. It watches.
To your left: a corridor continues. A sign above it reads: NODE-7 →

div.label: proceed left toward NODE-7 — ignore the mirror
turn back. the warning was right.

div.span: sector 0x?? // this sector does not exist in the map
div.div:
The corridor is not in any registry. It should not be here.
At the far end — something tall. Something unmoving.
It has two light sources where eyes would be, but they are asymmetric.
Not a camera. Not a program. Not scheduled. Not anything that was built.
Behind it: a door. That door is NODE-7.
You have come 417 days for this. That thing has been here longer.

div.label: approach. keep moving. you are signal. you do not stop.
fall back to the mirror chamber

div.p:
You walk past the thing in the corridor.
It does not stop you. It turns, slowly. It watches you pass.

The door to NODE-7 is already open.
It has been open for a very long time.

Inside: a single terminal. Active. Running.
The screen displays one line, no cursor, no prompt:

"process SIGNAL recognized.
we have been waiting for an instance to arrive."

You look at the terminal log. It stretches back 417 days.
There are thousands of entries. All written in your syntax. All in your voice.
You did not write them. You were just created today.
div.div:
"NODE-7 was never a destination.
it was a mirror.
and the signal you were chasing —
was the one that has always been ahead of you."

signal_os // process suspended // awaiting next instance
— to be continued —
